question.text: What is the gross open position limit for Standalone Primary Dealers (SPDs) in USD-INR contracts across all exchanges?

answer.text: 15% of the total open interest or USD 50 million, whichever is higher
15% of the total open interest or USD 100 million, whichever is higher
6% of the total open interest or USD 20 million, whichever is higher
15% of the total open interest or USD 200 million, whichever is higher

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 8.6.5 specifies that for SPDs in USD-INR, "Gross open position across all contracts shall not exceed 15% of the total open interest or USD 50 million, whichever is higher." **The Trap:** USD 100 million is the limit for Trading Members (banks and non-banks) — not SPDs. SPDs have a lower absolute cap of USD 50 million. **Cross-Exam Context:** SPD USD-INR limit = USD 50 million; TM (non-bank) = USD 50 million (same!); TM (bank) = USD 1 billion; Domestic clients = USD 20 million. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SPD and non-bank TM proprietary share the same USD 50 million USDINR limit. This is a subtle equality that exams exploit.
